blocks:
Sūrat al-Inshiqāq

Meccan, and it is 25 verses.

(١) إِذَا السَّماءُ انْشَقَّتْ ** (1) When the sky is split open, It was said (that it splits) with the clouds, due to His saying, the Almighty: يَوْمَ تَشَقَّقُ السَّماءُ بِالْغَمامِ. (25) The day the sky splits open with the clouds. And it was narrated from Ali (peace be upon him): It splits open from the Milky Way (المجرة). Al-Qummi said: (This occurs) on the Day of Resurrection (يوم القيامة). (٢) وَأَذِنَتْ لِرَبِّها ** (2) and gives ear to its Lord, And listened to Him, meaning: it submitted to the effect of His power when He willed its splitting, with the submission (انقياد) of the obedient one who gives ear (يأذن) to the commander (الأمير) and yields to him. وَحُقَّتْ ** (2) and is bound to do so! And it was made worthy (حقيقة) of listening and submission (الانقياد). (٣) وَإِذَا الْأَرْضُ مُدَّتْ ** (3) When the earth is stretched out, It is leveled by removing its mountains and hills (آكامها). In al-Majma', narrated from the Prophet (صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم), he said: "The earth will be replaced by other than the earth, and the heavens (will be replaced), so He will level it and stretch it out like the stretching of the Ukāẓī leather (الأديم العكاظي). You will see in it neither crookedness (عوجاً) nor unevenness (امتاً)."

(٤) وَأَلْقَتْ ما فِيها ** (4) and casts out what is within her, What is in her belly, of treasures and the dead. وَتَخَلَّتْ ** And she exerted her utmost effort in emptying herself until nothing remained within her interior. القمّيّ said: The earth will stretch out, then it will split open, and the people will emerge from it.

(٥) وَأَذِنَتْ لِرَبِّها ** (5) and gives ear to her Lord—and she is bound to do so. In casting out and emptying herself. وَحُقَّتْ ** For the permission [to obey]. And the answer to idha (إذا) is omitted.

(٦)** يا أَيُّهَا الْإِنْسانُ إِنَّكَ كادِحٌ إِلى رَبِّكَ كَدْحاً فَمُلاقِيهِ ** (6) O man! You are labouring toward your Lord laboriously, and you will encounter Him. Striving toward Him with striving, toward the encounter of His recompense.

(٧) فَأَمَّا مَنْ أُوتِيَ كِتابَهُ بِيَمِينِهِ. (7) Then as for him who is given his book in his right hand,

(٨)** فَسَوْفَ يُحاسَبُ حِساباً يَسِيراً ** (8) he will soon be accounted for with an easy reckoning, Easy, in which there is no scrutiny.

In al-Ma'ani, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him), he said: The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم said: "Every one who is reckoned with (مُحَاسَب) is punished (مُعَذَّب)." A speaker said to him: "O Messenger of Allah, then where is the saying of Allah, the Mighty and Majestic: فَسَوْفَ يُحاسَبُ حِساباً يَسِيراً ?" He said: "That is the presentation (العَرْض)," meaning the forgiveness (الصَّفْح). And in al-Jawami', it is narrated: That the easy reckoning (الحِسَاب الْيَسِير) is the rewarding for good deeds (الحَسَنَات) and the overlooking of bad deeds (السَّيِّئَات). And whoever is scrutinized (نُوقِشَ) in the reckoning is punished (عُذِّبَ).

(٩) وَيَنْقَلِبُ إِلى أَهْلِهِ مَسْرُوراً ** (9) and return to his family rejoicing. To his believing kin (عشيرته المؤمنين) and the wide-eyed maidens (الحور العين). (١٠) وَأَمَّا مَنْ أُوتِيَ كِتابَهُ وَراءَ ظَهْرِهِ ** (10) But as for him who is given his book from behind his back, It was said: meaning, his book is given to him in his left hand from behind his back. And it was said: his right hand is chained (تغلّ) to his neck, and his left hand is placed behind his back. (١١) فَسَوْفَ يَدْعُوا ثُبُوراً ** (11) he will soon call for perdition, He will wish for perdition (الثبور) and say, "O my perdition (وا ثبوراه)!" And it is destruction (الهلاك). And al-Qummi said: al-Thubūr (الثّبور) is woe (الويل). (١٢) وَيَصْلى سَعِيراً ** (12) and enter a blaze. And it was recited yuṣallā (يصلّي) with tashdīd (التّشديد), from al-taṣliyah (التصلية). (١٣) إِنَّهُ كانَ فِي أَهْلِهِ مَسْرُوراً ** (13) Indeed, he was rejoicing among his family. He was insolent (بطر) due to wealth and status (المال والجاه), heedless of the Hereafter (الآخرة). (١٤) إِنَّهُ ظَنَّ أَنْ لَنْ يَحُورَ ** (14) Indeed, he thought that he would never return. He will not return after he dies. (١٥) بَلى ** (15) Yes, indeed! He will return. إِنَّ رَبَّهُ كانَ بِهِ بَصِيراً ** (15) Indeed, his Lord has been watchful of him. Knowing his deeds (عالماً بأعماله), He will not neglect him, but rather He will return him and recompense him. (١٦) فَلا أُقْسِمُ بِالشَّفَقِ (16) I swear by the twilight glow, Al-Qummi said: The redness (الحمرة) after the setting of the sun. (١٧) وَاللَّيْلِ وَما وَسَقَ ** (17) and by the night and what it envelops, And what it gathers (جمعه) and conceals (ستره). (١٨) وَالْقَمَرِ إِذَا اتَّسَقَ ** (18) and by the moon when it is full, When it is gathered (اجتمع) and becomes a full moon (بدراً). (١٩) لَتَرْكَبُنَّ طَبَقاً عَنْ طَبَقٍ ** (19) that you will surely ride layer after layer. A state (حالاً) after a state, corresponding to its counterpart (مطابقة لأختها). In al-Ikmal, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): that you will surely ride layer after layer (19) means: the path (سير) of those who were before you. And in al-Jawami', narrated from him (peace be upon him): that you will surely ride (19) means: the ways (سنن) of those who were before you among the ancients, and their states (أحوالهم). And in al-Ihtijaj, narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him): Meaning, you will surely follow the path (سبيل) of the nations who were before you regarding the excuse (العذر) concerning the successors (الأوصياء) after the prophets (الأنبياء). And in al-Kafi and al-Qummi, narrated from Imam al-Baqir (peace be upon him): Has this nation not ridden layer after layer (طَبَقاً عَنْ طَبَقٍ) after its Prophet (صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم) concerning the matter of so-and-so, and so-and-so, and so-and-so? And al-Qummi says: that you will surely ride (19) means: the path (سبيل) of those who were before you, step for step (حذو النّعل بالنّعل) and feather for feather (القذّة بالقذّة). You will not deviate from their path, nor will it deviate a span for a span, or a cubit for a cubit, or an arm's length for an arm's length, until if those who were before you entered a lizard's hole (حجر ضبّ), you would enter it. They said: "Do you mean the Jews and the Christians, O Messenger of Allah (صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم)?" He said: "Then whom do I mean? The bonds (عُرَى) of Islam will be undone knot by knot (عروة عروة). The first thing you will undo from your religion will be trustworthiness (الأمانة), and the last of it will be the prayer (الصلاة)." And it was recited latarkabanna (لتركبنّ) with fatḥah (بالفتح), addressing man (الإنسان) based on the wording (اللّفظ).

(٢٠) فَما لَهُمْ لا يُؤْمِنُونَ. (20) What is the matter with them that they do not believe? (٢١) وَإِذا قُرِئَ عَلَيْهِمُ الْقُرْآنُ لا يَسْجُدُونَ ** (21) And when the Quran is recited to them, they do not prostrate? They do not humble themselves (لا يخضعون) or they do not prostrate (لا يسجدون) upon its recitation. In al-Jawami', narrated from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم: That one day وَاسْجُدْ وَاقْتَرِبْ** (Prostrate and draw near) was recited, so he prostrated, along with those believers who were with him, while Quraysh clapped above their heads and whistled. So [this Ayah] was revealed.

(٢٢) بَلِ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا يُكَذِّبُونَ. (22) Rather, the faithless deny [it]. (٢٣) وَاللهُ أَعْلَمُ بِما يُوعُونَ ** (23) and Allah knows best what they keep secret. meaning: what they conceal in their chests (صدورهم) of disbelief (الكفر) and enmity (العداوة). (٢٤) فَبَشِّرْهُمْ بِعَذابٍ أَلِيمٍ ** (24) So inform them of a painful punishment. This is said in mockery of them. (٢٥) إِلَّا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا وَعَمِلُوا الصَّالِحاتِ ** (25) except those who have faith and do righteous deeds. This is a disconnected exception (استثناء منقطع) or a connected exception (متّصل), and what is meant is those among them who repented and believed. لَهُمْ أَجْرٌ غَيْرُ مَمْنُونٍ ** (25) For them is an unfailing reward. meaning: uninterrupted (غير مقطوع) or not bestowed upon them as a favor (ممنون به).

The reward for reciting it (the Surah) was previously mentioned in Sūrat al-Infiṭār (سورة الإنفطار).
